text.title: On-Call Handoff Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of oncall-handoff-protocol enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Every handoff lists active incidents with: status, last action, next step, owner.
rule.rationale: Without an active incidents block, the incoming engineer rediscovers fire-fighting state from scratch.

rule.statement: Every handoff lists follow-up items with explicit dates / SLAs and an owner.
rule.rationale: Loose follow-ups become forgotten work and re-emerge as repeat incidents.

rule.statement: Incoming engineer acknowledges receipt in writing before shift hands over.
rule.rationale: Verbal acks evaporate; written ack is the closing artefact.

rule.statement: Even a quiet shift produces a one-line handoff ('no incidents, no follow-ups, no fatigue').
rule.rationale: Empty handoffs are still data; missing handoffs hide drift.

rule.statement: Outgoing engineer signals fatigue level (low/medium/high); high fatigue escalates to ops lead, not silent.
rule.rationale: Hidden fatigue compounds across shifts and produces incident-causing oversight.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, skip this methodology and route to the adjacent one referenced in Related.
rule.rationale: A skip rule keeps the decision tree closed: every branch ends at a real rule id.
